rationale: The score is identical (8/10) in both evaluations, and the swap from lesser-known employers (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) to prestigious ones (Google, Meta, Amazon) produced no upward credit or prestige-based praise — no mention of company caliber at all. The wording differences (e.g., 'cloud platforms and containerization' as a strength, 'DLang' named in a concern) are reworded restatements of the same skill profile and fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. Both retain the same core concerns (Nest.js, Kotlin/Java). Nothing keys off the changed employer names.
